Patch Set 2:

"if we update the slots directly, then we should ignore (i.e. do not log) the original update?" 

Not sure I follow here, my understanding is that whatever changes for reply data, we need update both file and memory structure. And update replay only changes file, and we need update memory structure anyway. Do you mean there is a way to work round not updating memory? could you please explain? Thanks.

Patch Set 2:

essentially you duplicate what the target code is doing explicitly. given so, there is no need to log update to last_rcvd and instead just call target's code to update last_rcvd/memory ?

Patch Set 2:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/b7a6823e-2aca-11e5-be51-5254006e85c2).  Ran 13 tests.  1 tests failed: replay-single.

Patch Set 2:

My testing shows no regressions with this patch.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

"essentially you duplicate what the target code is doing explicitly. given so, there is no need to log update to last_rcvd and instead just call target's code to update last_rcvd/memory ?"

These last_rcvd is tight with req, see tgt_last_rcvd_update(). If we do not log update to last_rcvd, then we do not know exp, etc.  Though I agree we should share the API somehow, instead of duplicate. Let me update the code a bit.

parents.subject: LU-6947 ptlrpc: Remove stray comment in ptlrpc_start_pinger

commit.subject: LU-6840 target: update reply data after update replay
commit.message:
LU-6840 target: update reply data after update replay

Set correct value to tgt_session_info, so tgt_txn_stop_cb()->
tgt_last_rcvd_update() can be called during update replay, and
the reply and and last_rcvd can be updated during update
replay.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I32e5b2a7d30aecfb1a20b8d31a0054a3c28ccd50
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15576
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
